title: polygala amarella var. uliginosa
intro: Polygala amarella var. uliginosa, also known as marshmilkwort, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to Europe. It is used in traditional medicine due to its antispasmodic, expectorant, and diuretic properties, which help in relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Polygala amarella var. uliginosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
